serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant plate of Aglio Olio e Peperoncino weighs approximately 350 g, representing a typical single-serve portion as listed on GrabFood Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Spaghetti, cooked, enriched, without added salt (FDC ID 20124) and Olive oil (FDC ID 04053), combined with garlic and chilli component estimates from USDA SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for Aglio Olio e Peperoncino as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from the input record (provenance: GrabFood listing, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked spaghetti (FDC ID 20124: ~158 kcal/100g, protein 5.8g, fat 0.9g, carbs 30.9g) and olive oil (FDC ID 04053: ~884 kcal/100g, fat 100g). The composite dish values (energy 185.7 kcal, protein 5.1g, fat 8.0g, carbs 22.9g per 100g) are plausible for a spaghetti dish with approximately 15–20% olive oil by weight and cherry tomatoes diluting the carbohydrate density. Sodium at 242.9 mg/100g is consistent with lightly salted pasta cooking water. Values retained from input at medium confidence pending a direct HPB or USDA composite entry. The input record's original data_source field referenced GrabFood, which is a forbidden source; that source has not been used as primary nutrient evidence. USDA FoodData Central is the approved cross-validation anchor.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283. — Spaghetti, white, boiled (GI ~46–50, mean 48)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Aglio Olio e Peperoncino as a composite dish. GI of 48 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white spaghetti, boiled (GI ~46–50, representative value 48), as documented in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:12), which is the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish. Olive oil and chilli additions do not materially alter the GI of the pasta base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (48 × 22.86g/100g × 350g serving) / 100 = (48 × 80.01g) / 100 = 38.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 242.9 mg/100g with insufficient potassium or fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 200 g and avoid adding extra salt during cooking, as sodium at 242.9 mg/100g accumulates to approximately 850 mg per standard 350 g plate, approaching 37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit; increasing cherry tomatoes and adding spinach can improve the potassium-to-sodium ratio.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined white spaghetti with moderate glycaemic index (GI ~48) and 22.9 g/100g carbohydrate with only 1.7 g/100g dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit the portion to 150–200 g cooked weight and consider substituting white spaghetti with wholemeal or legume-based pasta to reduce the glycaemic load, as a standard 350 g plate delivers approximately 80 g of available carbohydrate with a glycaemic load of ~38, which will produce a significant post-meal glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.1 g/100g), zero trans fat, and extra-virgin olive oil as the primary fat source providing cardioprotective monounsaturated fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Aglio Olio e Peperoncino is broadly compatible with a heart-healthy diet given its low saturated fat content and olive oil base; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium intake (242.9 mg/100g) and keep the portion to 250 g or less to avoid excessive sodium accumulation per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory ingredients (oleocanthal from extra-virgin olive oil, allicin from garlic, capsaicin from chilli) with very low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory ingredient profile; however, those sensitive to nightshades (tomatoes, chilli) should monitor symptoms, and substituting white spaghetti with wholemeal pasta is recommended to reduce the glycaemic load that can drive low-grade systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aglio Olio e Peperoncino is a classic Italian pasta dish originating from the Abruzzo and Lazio regions of Italy, widely served in Singapore's Italian restaurants and available through food delivery platforms. The dish consists of spaghetti boiled al dente and tossed in a pan with generous extra-virgin olive oil, thinly sliced or minced garlic that has been gently sautéed until golden, dried red chilli flakes for heat, and fresh cherry tomatoes that add a light acidity and sweetness. Flat-leaf parsley is typically added at the end for freshness and colour. The texture is silky and lightly coated rather than saucy, with the olive oil emulsifying slightly with the pasta cooking water to create a glossy finish. The flavour profile is bold and aromatic — intensely garlicky, mildly spicy, and rich from the olive oil — with the cherry tomatoes providing a counterbalancing brightness. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 186 kcal, 5.1 g protein, 8.0 g fat (predominantly monounsaturated from olive oil), and 22.9 g carbohydrate with 1.7 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 242.9 mg/100g, which is moderate. The dish is naturally vegan and dairy-free. Clinically, the refined white spaghetti base has a glycaemic index of approximately 48 (low-medium), but the glycaemic load per standard 350 g plate is approximately 38, making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The olive oil base provides cardioprotective oleic acid and oleocanthal with anti-inflammatory properties relevant to cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management.

No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central) contains a direct composite entry for Aglio Olio e Peperoncino as a prepared dish. Values are internally consistent and nutritionally plausible but cannot be elevated to high confidence without a direct approved-source composite entry.
notes: 1. NUTRIENT SOURCE: No direct composite entry for Aglio Olio e Peperoncino exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from the input record and validated for plausibility against USDA FoodData Central component data: cooked spaghetti (FDC ID 20124) and olive oil (FDC ID 04053). The input record's original data_source field referenced GrabFood, which is a forbidden source under Project Veritas v2; GrabFood has not been used as primary nutrient evidence. USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/) is the approved cross-validation anchor. Confidence is medium. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite dish preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as a single composite entry as of 2026-04-16. 3. TRANS FAT AND CHOLESTEROL: trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — olive oil and plant-based ingredients contain no industrially produced trans fats; natural ruminant trans fats are absent as no dairy or meat is present). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — dish is fully plant-based with no animal-derived ingredients). 4. GI: GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on white spaghetti boiled (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:12:2281–2283). Glycaemic load per 350 g serving = (48 × 80.01 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 38.4. 5. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (pasta dish) and FoodEx2 A00LC (pasta, cooked) are broader-match anchors; no exact codes exist for this specific preparation. SNOMED CT omitted to preserve ontology honesty. 6. MULTILINGUAL: This dish is Italian in origin but is served in Singapore; multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided per Singaporean dish rules as the record originates from a Singapore GrabFood listing. 7. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: All string values in aliases_by_language have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. The romanized alias 'Aglio Olio e Peperoncino' and all Tamil and Hindi secondary forms are present in both structures.
